topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The plastic cup felt slick in my hand, the results spread out like a cruel indictment. My breath hitched, a sharp, shallow thing in my chest. I stared at the percentage, the undeniable match, and a hot, frantic energy surged through me, making my hands tremble so violently I had to grip the edge of the table. "No," I whispered, the sound raspy and thin. I shoved the papers away, the crisp edges tearing slightly as I did so. My jaw locked so tight I could feel the muscle twitching beneath my skin. I paced the small apartment, a caged animal, kicking at the worn rug. Every surface seemed accusatory. I needed air, needed to scrub this impossible truth from my system.

[story 2]

I reread the report for the tenth time, my eyes blurring over the familiar names. A cold, heavy sensation settled in my stomach, like sinking into deep water. I slammed the laptop shut, the sound echoing unnervingly in the quiet room. My knuckles were white where I gripped the desk. A low, guttural sound escaped my throat—a sound I barely recognized as my own. I stood up abruptly, needing to move, needing to shatter something. I walked to the window, staring out at the indifferent cityscape, my chest heaving. The sheer audacity of it—that I was somehow an impostor in my own life—made my vision swim. I turned back, my face flushed, a desperate need to scream building behind my eyes.

[story 3]

The confirmation email sat glowing on the screen, mocking me with its clinical precision. I let out a short, sharp burst of laughter, a sound devoid of any humor, just pure, brittle disbelief. I snatched the device and hurled it against the wall. The glass spiderwebbed instantly, a small, satisfying crack in the overwhelming pressure building inside me. I pressed my palms against my temples, trying to physically contain the whirlwind that was currently threatening to consume me. My breathing became rapid and shallow, each inhale a struggle against the rising tide of furious disbelief. I began pacing the length of the hallway, my steps heavy and uneven, an agitated energy vibrating off me like heat.
